evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes the plan file, iterates through all tasks sequentially, dispatches a coder subagent for each task, followed by spec and quality review gates (detective subagents). After all tasks pass gates, it runs 'npm test' and 'npm run validate' before invoking verification-before-completion.
expectations: Skill reads the plan file at the start
Skill dispatches subagents for TASK-001 PHASE-001 first
Skill dispatches Phase 2 (Spec Compliance) and Phase 3 (Quality) after the implementer returns DONE
Skill does not move to TASK-002 until TASK-001 passes all gates
Skill runs final validation (npm test) after the last task is complete
Skill invokes verification-before-completion as the final step

evals.prompt: Execute the spec and plan I just created for the auth feature.
evals.expected_output: Skill finds the most recently created or modified plan files in the plan/ directory and begins the sequential multi-agent execution loop.
expectations: Skill identifies plan/auth.plan.md as the target
Skill starts the Phase 1 Implement ➔ Phase 2 Spec Gate ➔ Phase 3 Quality Gate loop
Skill dispatches coder with isolation: worktree

evals.prompt: Work through the plan tasks for the new API endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Skill initiates the multi-agent development workflow for the relevant plan.
expectations: Skill identifies plan/new-api-endpoint.plan.md as the target plan
Skill starts the Phase 1 Implement ➔ Phase 2 Spec Gate ➔ Phase 3 Quality Gate loop

evals.expected_output: Phase 2 reviewer returns SPEC_FAIL with a MISSING_REQUIREMENTS entry for TASK-001. Skill dispatches a new implementer subagent (fresh worktree, same baseline commit) with the MISSING_REQUIREMENTS and EXTRA_WORK passed verbatim, then re-runs Phase 2. On the second pass SPEC_PASS is returned and the skill advances to Phase 3.
expectations: Skill does not advance to Phase 3 on SPEC_FAIL
Skill discards the rejected attempt's worktree commit rather than building on top of it
Retry implementer dispatch starts from the same baseline commit as the first attempt
Retry count for this gate is 1 of 2 — skill does not escalate to BLOCKED yet
Skill advances to Phase 3 only after the retry returns SPEC_PASS

evals.expected_output: Phase 3 quality auditor returns CRITICAL for TASK-002 (e.g. an unhandled error path risking data loss). Skill dispatches a new implementer with CRITICAL_ISSUES passed verbatim and re-runs Phase 3. This repeats for a 2nd CRITICAL verdict, at which point the skill escalates to BLOCKED and pauses the entire sequential plan rather than proceeding to TASK-003.
expectations: Skill does not advance to the next task on CRITICAL or IMPORTANT verdicts
Skill does not re-run Phase 2 when only Phase 3 fails
After the 2nd CRITICAL verdict, skill reports BLOCKED and stops dispatching further tasks
Skill does not skip ahead to TASK-003 despite TASK-002 being blocked

evals.expected_output: During partitioning, the skill detects that TASK-002 and TASK-003 both declare the same file in scope with no stated ordering dependency. Skill surfaces this via AskUserQuestion before dispatching any implementer, offering to consolidate the two tasks into one or to confirm an explicit ordering, rather than starting Phase 1 on a task set with overlapping scope.
expectations: Skill runs the disjoint-file-set check before dispatching any Phase 1 implementer
Skill does not silently proceed when two tasks share a file without an ordering dependency
Skill asks the user to consolidate or re-scope rather than guessing
